acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_f001] Pass if the revised version of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:143 makes the Gloria-to-apartment trip about fifteen minutes, or clearly states a reason the trip takes longer; fail if the chapter still presents a direct Gloria-to-apartment drive of twenty-five minutes.

[chapter_01_f002] Pass if, before the equivalent of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:167, the revised chapter shows at least one concrete disruption that plausibly causes the extra half-tab in Harry's cup; fail if the reader still learns why Carmen miscounted only in retrospective explanation after Harry points it out.

[chapter_01_f003] Pass if the revised version of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:17 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:25 establishes Lola's warmth and confusion without reusing the ordered beat-chain of tiredness -> chicken -> day check -> husband's cooking; fail if that scaffold is still recognizable on the page.

[chapter_01_f004] Pass if the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:147, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:187, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:189 no longer end on abstract thesis phrasing and instead remain anchored in sensory detail, action, or immediate interiority; fail if those beats still culminate in summary taglines that could be lifted out as slogans.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised version of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:143 makes the Gloria-to-apartment trip about fifteen minutes, or clearly states a reason the trip takes longer; fail if the chapter still presents a direct Gloria-to-apartment drive of twenty-five minutes.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:143 states that Carmen's drive from Gloria's house to her apartment takes twenty-five minutes.
findings.problem: This contradicts the continuity sheet's established distance of roughly fifteen minutes between Gloria's house and Carmen's apartment. In a chapter built around commute math, schedule margins, and financial precision, a wrong travel time is not cosmetic; it damages the factual pressure the chapter depends on.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the post-Gloria transit beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:143 so the trip matches the established fifteen-minute distance, or add an explicit intervening stop/delay on-page. If any downstream commute math in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:147 depends on the longer drive, align that passage too.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, before the equivalent of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:167, the revised chapter shows at least one concrete disruption that plausibly causes the extra half-tab in Harry's cup; fail if the reader still learns why Carmen miscounted only in retrospective explanation after Harry points it out.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:35 presents the med pass as routine; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:79 shows general understaffing pressure; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:169 explains only after Harry's correction that Carmen miscounted because she was behind from doing the 6 PM turns herself.
findings.problem: The chapter's key turn depends on Carmen making a tramadol-count error, but the decisive last-mile pressure is retro-explained instead of dramatized before the mistake lands. The result is a slightly arranged feel: the chapter tells us she was overloaded, but it does not show the concrete interruption, rush, or divided attention that produces the extra half-tab before Harry catches it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Seed the causal chain inside the work sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:79, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:159 by adding a specific operational disruption before Harry's room: an interrupted count, a resident need that forces Carmen to resume mid-task, or a turn that leaves her rushing back to the cart. Do not leave the explanation confined to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:169 after Harry has already exposed the error.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised version of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:17 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:25 establishes Lola's warmth and confusion without reusing the ordered beat-chain of tiredness -> chicken -> day check -> husband's cooking; fail if that scaffold is still recognizable on the page.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:17 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:21 reproduce the style-bible calibration sequence almost beat for beat: 'Mija, you look tired' plus chicken, 'what day is it,' and Tom's cooking.
findings.problem: Lola's first exchange is not merely style-adjacent; it is conspicuously built from calibration-only example material. Because this is her introduction in chapter 1, the borrowing damages voice integrity on first read and makes the dialogue feel generated from notes rather than spoken by a character.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite Lola's opening exchange from scratch at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:21, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:25. Keep the intended qualities from the style bible -- warmth, indirectness, mild performed confusion, story-drift -- but replace the borrowed verbal sequence and turn-shape entirely.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:147, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:187, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:189 no longer end on abstract thesis phrasing and instead remain anchored in sensory detail, action, or immediate interiority; fail if those beats still culminate in summary taglines that could be lifted out as slogans.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:147, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:187, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:189 each resolve concrete material into abstract coda language ('It was a condition she'd become,' 'The math found everything,' 'A good moment was...,' 'Assessment. Expectation.').
findings.problem: A recurring prose habit turns strong sensory or procedural observation into detachable thesis lines. The chapter is most authoritative when it stays in bodies, objects, and work; these abstract codas sound workshopped rather than lived, and they loosen the close-third grip the style bible calls for.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the paragraph endings around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:147, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:187, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:189 so the meaning is carried by observable detail, Carmen-specific inference, or silence rather than aphoristic summary. Preserve the underlying point, but land it through scene texture instead of detachable taglines.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
